title: Design Patterns
subtitle: Elements of Reusable Object-Oriented Software
author: Erich Gamma, Richard Helm, Ralph Johnson, and John Vlissides
themes: Reusable solutions to common software design problems
The catalog of 23 foundational design patterns organized by purpose
Object-oriented design principles: composition over inheritance, program to interfaces
Patterns as a shared vocabulary for software design communication
The relationship between patterns, frameworks, and architectures
awards: One of the most influential programming books ever written
Cited in tens of thousands of research papers and technical books
Named one of the 100 most influential books by several technology publications
excerpt: Design Patterns: Elements of Reusable Object-Oriented Software introduced the concept of design patterns to software engineering. The Gang of Four cataloged 23 patterns that have become the foundation of modern object-oriented design, providing a shared vocabulary that every developer needs to know.
